title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: An exploration of the two systems that drive the way we think — fast intuitive thinking and slow deliberate thinking.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of humanity's creation and evolution that explores how biology and history have defined us.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A proven framework for improving every day through tiny changes in behavior that compound into remarkable results.

title: The Design of Everyday Things
author: Don Norman
description: A powerful primer on how and why some products satisfy customers while others frustrate them.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world, offering strategies for cultivating deep concentration.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas & Andrew Hunt
description: A timeless guide to software development that covers topics from personal responsibility to career development.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor E. Frankl
description: A memoir and philosophical work exploring how meaning can be found even in the most brutal conditions.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: Personal writings of the Roman Emperor on Stoic philosophy, self-discipline, and resilience.

title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
author: Eric Jorgenson
description: A collection of Naval's wisdom and experience on wealth creation and finding happiness.

title: Essentialism: The Disciplined Pursuit of Less
author: Greg McKeown
description: A systematic discipline for discerning what is absolutely essential and eliminating everything that is not.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A memoir of a woman who grew up in a survivalist family and went on to earn a PhD from Cambridge.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Liu Cixin
description: A hard science fiction novel that imagines humanity's first contact with an alien civilization.

title: Factfulness
author: Hans Rosling
description: Ten reasons we're wrong about the world and why things are better than you think.

title: Range: Why Generalists Triumph in a Specialized World
author: David Epstein
description: Makes the case for breadth of experience in a world that increasingly prizes specialization.

title: The Mom Test
author: Rob Fitzpatrick
description: How to talk to customers and learn if your business is a good idea when everyone is lying to you.
